label: style: archimedean
user_description: i see 4 thin curved lines spinning at a steady rate around a center point. the lines are cycling through nice colors and pulsating with the beat. the lines fade out at the ends, and don't ever reach the edges of the screen and is very far from reaching the left or right edges occupying maybe 40% of the sceen space. it's pleasant to look at, but so subdued it's a little boring. felt nothing.

label: style: fermat
user_description: 4 very soft and curved lines rotating very slowly around the center point. each line is glowing a different shade of pink and is thicker at the origin point than at the tail end. the colors are very bright and impossible to see through at the fat ends of the lines, but quickly become translucent after tapering off until they fade out of sight. the lines pulsate with the beat. there's a large blank space in the center of this spiral. doesn't reach far enough either. a little boring.

label: style: logarithmic
user_description: a flickering rainbow tunnel made up from thin lines rotating around a point, but each line has a smudged kink in it that kind of blends it into the next line over, leading to an effect that makes additional lines appear out of the smudges, giving the tunnel an almost web-like melted appearance. it's pretty cool for being just some thin lines. the colors push out from the center as the lines rotate and it is very easy to see through. the effects of the pulsing are amplified because this spiral takes up a lot of the screen with a high density of flickering lines, and it's easy to feel pulled in by such a clearly defined tunnel. it's not reaching the far edges though, and fade to near invisibilty by the time it reaches the top or bottom of the screen.

label: style: golden
user_description: very similar to the previous one, but spinning slower and with bolder colors

label: style: fibonacci
user_description: 3 incredibly thin neon pink lines round in a very tight spiral with way too big of a center point. the flickering in the lines becomes more visible at the spread out, but the lines barely spread any wider at all, maybe only a few times thicker at the end than they are at the start. the lines also fad out quickly. this spiral is and thing and boring and i hate it

label: style: hyperbolic
user_description: if there's a difference between this one and 3 i can't notice it.

label: style: lituus
user_description: same as 5

label: style: vortex
user_description: i see a spiral made from 2 thick opaque pink lines that are very steady in the center and flicker wildly at the edge of the spiral. in addition to this 2 lines it looks like there's a second set of the same 2 lines rotating at a different speed interwoven with the other. i particularly like the width of these lines, but they taper off into flickering nothing far too prematurely. there's also variance in the color, its changing shades of pink. this is almost a cool spiral.

label: style: galaxy
user_description: i see 8 lines i think it is, emanating from the center. these lines are a lot thicker than all but style 7 so far, but i could consider these like the normal size of a spiral line, if that makes sense. that being said with 8 lines of this thickness coming from the center, it's very densely packed in the center. the colors don't cycle, it's static pink in the middle, and the lines end in different colors, and each line has a bright strip in the center of it. again, doesn't reach the edges at all. hard to look away from the center on this oely even with this relative low opacity.

label: style: kaleidoscope
user_description: a flickering kaleidoscope made out of soft rose pine colored lights. the pulsing is very strong on this spiral. i love the kaleidoscope so much i wish it had even more going on, because it's only like one shape at a time going through the "tunnel". sometimes 2, but mostly just 1 at a time.

label: style: interference
user_description: i see 2 points, one of them is orbiting the other and both look like they're slurping up the glowing, flickering, ethereal lines coming out of the ether. each point is pulling in 4 lines. there is VERY little on the screen, and they're very transparent. this one could be interesting, but in reality, there's far too little going on visually here.

label: style: tunnel_dream
user_description: it's tunnel_dream again. idk what else to say about it.

label: style: rose
user_description: i see a series of quivering ovoid lines rotating at offset speeds and cycling through rose pine colors. the spiral "blades" i call them remind me very much of the burner on a gas cooktop. at the center where the blades meet there's a glowing circle behind the blades... this spiral is really fucking cool honestly it does so many cool effects just from the rotating offsets... the way they overlap with each over blends the colors and that color blending naturally responds to the pulsing effect... love this spiral, which is also didn't fade out prematurely.

label: style: moire
user_description: i see 8 very thin lines, 2 pink, 2 orange, 2 blue, 2 gold. the lines are structured to deliberately interect in many places, and wher they intersect, they form glowing points. this spiral has a bit of novely to it, but these thin, low opacity lines don't demand my attention.

label: style: phyllotaxis
user_description: i see tunnel dream again idk

label: style: polar_rose
user_description: it's tunnel dream again but this time it's rose pine colors

label: style: starburst
user_description: tunnel_dream

label: style: cobwebs
user_description: i see 5 or 6 solid concentric rings. i see 16 lines evenly spaced connecting every ring. bright concentric rings emanate from the center of the spiral and scroll across it's surface, looking like moving dots on each line, and revealing themselves as a full circle once it reaches the first ring. it's a cool effect, but this spiral doesn't spin and it has a visible seam on the left side.

label: style: strange_attractor
user_description: this is the worst fucking spiral i've ever seen. it's like a morphing strobing single glob of wax from a lava lamp. idk what the fuck this is supposed to be. i'm annoyed looking at it.

label: style: flow_field
user_description: i see some vague wisps of flickering colors on the screen. this is similar to style 10 actually, but significantly worse. no idea what's going on. hate it.

label: style: sacred_geometry
user_description: fuck this shit. jesus christ. it's just a full screen field of blindingly white hexagons that quiver to the beat and have a blatant grid of colors applied to only the edges of the hexagons. this is a torture device of the highest order.

label: style: recursive_fractal
user_description: wow it's fucking nothing. literally. there's nothing here.

label: entrain: 0.0
user_description: it does. it's obvious and it's synced.

label: entrain: 0.15
user_description: it pulses, it's obvious, it's synced.

label: entrain: 0.3
user_description: it pulses, it's so obvious it's basically turning all the way off and on. it's synced.

label: entrain: 0.5
user_description: it pulses, it's even more obvious, it's synced.

label: entrain: 0.7
user_description: it pulses, i can't tell the difference between .5 or .7

label: entrain: 0.85
user_description: it pulses. i can't tell the difference from .5

label: entrain: 1.0
user_description: yes, obviously, it's basically synced with the center text at this point.

label: trail: decay=0.0 mode=none
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=none
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=none
user_description: there's like a ghost of a 2nd spiral in the background that's offset completely from the first

label: trail: decay=0.95 mode=none
user_description: same as previous but the background spiral is a little more visible

label: trail: decay=0.0 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: if i crank the opacity up to max i can see it very subtly, spinning in the opposite direction in the background.

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: it's spinning in the opposite direction yep

label: trail: decay=0.95 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: that sure is almost like it's two spirals going in opposite directions again

label: trail: decay=0.0 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: almost imperceptibly, yes.

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: i can see the background opposite spiral without trying at this level

label: trail: decay=0.95 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: uh huh

label: trail: decay=0.0 mode=rotational_smear
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=rotational_smear
user_description: very subtly, if i look for it

label: style: galaxy
user_description: dramatically improved with the vignette removal, an acceptable spiral now.

label: style: archimedean
user_description: vignette removal improved this one but not a meaningful amount, still weak

label: style: interference
user_description: wow this one finally makes sense and i appreciate it for what it is. the flickering lines being pulled towards the dual focal points being full length.

label: style: electric
user_description: delete this

label: style: vortex
user_description: this is quite a lot better now, interesting.

label: style: fibonacci
user_description: garbage

label: style: spirograph
user_description: similar to rose in some ways, but it's ultra washed out. you can really only see the center, and a little bit near the tips of the loops. just don't like this.

label: style: fermat
user_description: the thick lines that gradually get thinner and each line being a distinct color makes this one actually pretty unique

label: style: superformula
user_description: superformula is cool but it gets really blurry really quickly based on arm thickness. it's mid.

label: style: liminal
user_description: this one reacts to chaos so well, but with low chaos is just a boring spiral with way too much glow towards the center

label: style: resonant
user_description: not worth salvaging.

label: style: nebula
user_description: i see a fluid array of distorted concentric rainbow colored lines. very relaxing. huge glow up with the vignette removal.

label: style: bifurcate
user_description: i see worthless trash get rid of this.

label: style: cobwebs
user_description: i kind of want the old cobwebs back but spinning. this is forgettable and the vignette removal makes the concentric ring overlay feel awkward.

label: style: strange_attractor
user_description: this one actually kind of was enhanced by the vignette as well, it is kind of boring seeing the whole pattern the whole time, makes it too predictable

label: style: sacred_geometry
user_description: i see a diamond pattern of sacred geometry rotating. simple. fine.

label: style: recursive_fractal
user_description: can you go back to the original i wanna see it without the vignette. this is only just ok.

label: style: potter_tunnel
user_description: wow i love this. the tunnel, but somna rainbow... the "hole" might be a little too wide, but this perfect otherwise

label: style: fractal_scale
user_description: this one didn't translate as well, the leftside seam is there too. i think the problem is it's too transparent.

label: style: neuro_vortex
user_description: this one is cool, but it didn't survive the translation. it's like concentric flickering rings and each ring has a lot of complexity with bands made up of a gajillion lenticular lines. i don't know how the fuck to describe this honestly. it's like 80% of the way there but i have no idea how to define it.

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=none
user_description: yes and it's INTENSE. it can't go over this.

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=none
user_description: oh god my eyes

label: trail: decay=0.95 mode=none
user_description: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: upper limit allowed

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=alpha_decay
user_description: too much

label: trail: decay=0.0 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: no

label: trail: decay=0.6 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: yes, and it's overbearing

label: trail: decay=0.85 mode=radial_zoom
user_description: i'm detecting a fucking pattern here
